dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8480 IH: Protect our Pets Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Gottheimer (for himself and Mr. Lawler) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to increase the penalties for animal crushing, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protect our Pets Act.
Subsection (c) of section 48 of title 18, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

subsection.text: Whoever violates this section shall be—

paragraph.text: in the case of a first offense, fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both; and
in the case of a second or subsequent offense, fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 15 years, or both.

section.header: Abandonment or endangerment of animals on Federal land prohibited

subsection.text: Chapter 3 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 48 the following:
Whoever, in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce, or on Federal land, knowingly abandons a domesticated animal or commits an act of violence that endangers a domesticated animal, shall be—

paragraph.text: in the case of a first offense, fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both; and
in the case of a second or subsequent offense, fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 15 years, or both.

subsection.text: This section does not apply with regard to any conduct that is—

paragraph.text: a customary and normal veterinary, agricultural husbandry, or other animal management practice;
the slaughter of animals for food;
hunting, trapping, fishing, a sporting activity not otherwise prohibited by Federal law, predator control, or pest control;
necessary to protect the life or property of a person; or
performed as part of euthanizing an animal.

subsection.text: In this section, the term domesticated animal means a living non-human mammal, bird, reptile, or amphibian, whether kept for pleasure or commercial purposes.
The table of section sections for chapter 3 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 48 the following:
